title: Listening - Part 3: Restoring Old Objects
description: You are going to hear five extracts in which people talk about an old object they repaired or restored. For questions 19–23, choose from the list A–H why each speaker decided to restore the object. Use the letters once only. There are three letters that you do not need to use.
instructions: You are going to hear five extracts in which people talk about an old object they repaired or restored. For questions 19–23, choose from the list A–H why each speaker decided to restore the object. Use the letters once only. There are three letters that you do not need to use.

extracts.context: Five people talk about restoring an old object.
extracts.audio_script: Speaker 1 Woman: My grandfather had a wooden toolbox that he carried everywhere when he was repairing things around the house. After he died, it ended up in a garage, covered in dust, with one broken handle. [19]I cleaned it and repaired the handle because seeing it again brought back memories of following him around as a child and being allowed to hand him tools.[/19] I don't actually need a toolbox that size, but I keep it on a shelf and store small photographs inside it.||Speaker 2 Man: Our kitchen chair developed a loose leg, and my flatmate immediately suggested putting it outside and ordering a new one. It wasn't an expensive chair, so buying another would have been easy. [20]I felt uncomfortable throwing away a whole piece of furniture because of one repairable fault, so I watched some instructions online and fixed the joint instead.[/20] It took one afternoon and a few basic materials. The chair is now solid again, and it has encouraged us to repair other household items before replacing them.||Speaker 3 Woman: I needed a desk for my new flat and found an old one at a local market. It was scratched and the drawers stuck, while new desks online looked neat and ready to use. [21]I chose to restore the older desk because it was made from solid wood and felt much stronger than the lightweight new furniture I could afford.[/21] After sanding it and treating the drawers, it looks better than I expected. It was hard work, but I think it will last for many years.||Speaker 4 Man: I bought an old bicycle cheaply at a market even though I already had one that worked. My parents couldn't understand why I wanted a rusty bike with flat tyres. [22]I had been curious about how bicycles work, and restoring one seemed a practical way to teach myself how to replace parts, adjust brakes and look after a bike properly.[/22] I made plenty of mistakes and had to ask for advice, but once it was finished, riding something I had rebuilt myself felt brilliant.||Speaker 5 Woman: My parents were celebrating their anniversary and planned a dinner with old family photographs on display. In a cupboard I found a large silver photo frame that had belonged to them when they first married, but it was badly marked. [23]I polished and repaired it so we could put their original wedding photograph in it as part of the anniversary celebration.[/23] They recognised it immediately and became quite emotional. I'm not sure they noticed the other decorations after that, which made all the effort worthwhile.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_1
dialogue.text: My grandfather had a wooden toolbox that he carried everywhere when he was repairing things around the house. After he died, it ended up in a garage, covered in dust, with one broken handle. I cleaned it and repaired the handle because seeing it again brought back memories of following him around as a child and being allowed to hand him tools. I don't actually need a toolbox that size, but I keep it on a shelf and store small photographs inside it.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_2
dialogue.text: Our kitchen chair developed a loose leg, and my flatmate immediately suggested putting it outside and ordering a new one. It wasn't an expensive chair, so buying another would have been easy. I felt uncomfortable throwing away a whole piece of furniture because of one repairable fault, so I watched some instructions online and fixed the joint instead. It took one afternoon and a few basic materials. The chair is now solid again, and it has encouraged us to repair other household items before replacing them.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_3
dialogue.text: I needed a desk for my new flat and found an old one at a local market. It was scratched and the drawers stuck, while new desks online looked neat and ready to use. I chose to restore the older desk because it was made from solid wood and felt much stronger than the lightweight new furniture I could afford. After sanding it and treating the drawers, it looks better than I expected. It was hard work, but I think it will last for many years.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_4
dialogue.text: I bought an old bicycle cheaply at a market even though I already had one that worked. My parents couldn't understand why I wanted a rusty bike with flat tyres. I had been curious about how bicycles work, and restoring one seemed a practical way to teach myself how to replace parts, adjust brakes and look after a bike properly. I made plenty of mistakes and had to ask for advice, but once it was finished, riding something I had rebuilt myself felt brilliant.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_5
dialogue.text: My parents were celebrating their anniversary and planned a dinner with old family photographs on display. In a cupboard I found a large silver photo frame that had belonged to them when they first married, but it was badly marked. I polished and repaired it so we could put their original wedding photograph in it as part of the anniversary celebration. They recognised it immediately and became quite emotional. I'm not sure they noticed the other decorations after that, which made all the effort worthwhile.

options.text: It reminded them of a relative.
They needed it for a special event.
They wanted to learn a new skill.
It was better made than new alternatives.
They wanted to avoid creating waste.
They intended to lend it to someone.
It fitted perfectly in their home.

questions.speaker: Speaker 1
questions.explanation: The toolbox was restored because it carries important memories of the speaker's grandfather.

questions.speaker: Speaker 2
questions.explanation: The speaker repaired the chair to avoid unnecessarily throwing usable furniture away.

questions.speaker: Speaker 3
questions.explanation: The speaker restored an older desk because its quality was superior to affordable new furniture.

questions.speaker: Speaker 4
questions.explanation: The speaker wanted a repair project that would teach bicycle maintenance skills.

questions.speaker: Speaker 5
questions.explanation: The old frame was restored so it could be used for the parents' anniversary celebration.
